disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain began at the same time as menstrual bleeding started this morning; cramps recur with most periods
trigger_question_examples: When exactly did the pain start compared with your period?
Does this happen with your periods, and did today's pain begin when the bleeding started?
Is the pain linked to your menstrual cycle at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, it started this morning right when my period came on. I usually get cramps with my periods, this is just so much worse this time.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reproductive-age woman, sexually active, not using reliable contraception; pregnancy cannot be excluded and no pregnancy test has been done
trigger_question_examples: I need to ask a sensitive question that helps me keep you safe — is there any chance you could be pregnant?
When was your last period before this one, and could you be pregnant — are you sexually active and using any contraception?
Sometimes serious causes of this pain relate to pregnancy — is there any possibility you're pregnant?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I guess there's a chance. I'm not on the pill or anything and I haven't done a test. I just assumed this was my period because the bleeding came.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: It's just my period, it comes every month.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Intractable vomiting with complete inability to tolerate oral fluids or oral analgesia over several hours
trigger_question_examples: Are you able to keep any fluids down at all?
How many times have you vomited, and can you drink water?
Have you managed to keep any food, water or medicine down today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing stays down. I've been sick several times and even a sip of water comes straight back up. That's why the paracetamol didn't work — I threw it up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, no abnormal vaginal discharge, no urinary symptoms, no pain with intercourse
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any fever, chills, or unusual vaginal discharge?
Any burning when you wee, or any smelly or unusual discharge?
Have you had a temperature or any pain passing urine?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, no fever that I've noticed, no weird discharge, and it doesn't hurt to wee.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain is central and low, radiating to the lower back and both thighs — not localised or lateralised to one side
trigger_question_examples: Is the pain more on one side, or is it in the middle?
Can you point to where it's worst — is it low in the middle, or off to the right or left?
Has the pain moved to one particular spot, like the lower right side?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's right in the middle, low down, and it spreads into my back and the tops of both legs. It's not really on one side.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms began roughly 12 months after menarche with progressively cyclical pattern; today's episode is exceptionally severe
trigger_question_examples: How long have you been getting these period cramps — did they start soon after your periods began?
Are these cramps something new, or have you had them for years?
When did the painful periods first start for you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They started maybe a year after I first got my periods, so a few years now. I get them most months but this is on another level.
